format.title: Vibrant, educational title for this session

previousSessionRecap: Short recap points from the previous lesson
learningOutcomes: Specific session outcome phrased for teacher delivery
teachingSequence: Teacher-facing lesson flow with explanation steps

lessonBlocks.title: Introduction
boardWork: What to write on the board

conceptFlow.relationshipWithFuture: Connection with future learning without teaching it

endOfClassRecap.prompt: A cell is the ________ unit of life.

nextSessionBridge: How today connects to the next lesson

studentLessonNotes.title: Student lesson note title
studentLessonNotes.sessionOverview: Simple overview of what this session covers
learningObjectives: After this lesson you will be able to...
easyToRemember: Simple memory line for the concept

sections.heading: Main idea
sections.whyItMatters: Why students should care about this concept
sections.detailedExplanation: A slightly deeper but student-friendly explanation

fillInTheBlanks.prompt: A ________ is the basic unit of life.

veryShortAnswerQuestions.question: What is a cell?
veryShortAnswerQuestions.answer: The basic structural and functional unit of life.

format.introduction: A exciting 3-5 minute hook, inquiry question, or classroom starter

ppt.title: Presentation title
ppt.presentationGoal: What this PPT helps achieve in the session

licensingNotes: Use reusable or public domain assets only.
assetSearchPlan.fallbackStrategy: Prefer SVG diagrams when external imagery is not needed.

licenseChecklist: Verify reusable license before final export.
presentationWarnings: Do not include untaught future-session content.

slides.visualPlan: What visual should appear and why

svgDiagram.title: Diagram title
svgDiagram.type: flowchart | timeline | labelled diagram | comparison chart
instructions: How the diagram should communicate the concept

keyInformation: Student-facing study note or revision point

longAnswer.question: Long-answer question
rubric: Credit labelled example or diagram if relevant

generalMarkingGuidance: Award marks point-wise for each valid idea.
Accept equivalent scientific wording if the meaning is correct.
For long answers, reward structure, explanation, and relevant examples.

assignment.answerKey: Sample answer or response key for teacher reference

messages.content: Return only valid JSON that matches the requested schema. Do not include markdown or explanations.
